message.content: Refactor the database layer to use a connection pool. Also some tests broke after yesterday's changes.

aiTitle: Refactor DB connection pool and fix tests

content.text: I'll check the current DB implementation and then look at the failing tests.
Single client, no pool. I'll refactor to use pg.Pool.
DB refactored. Now let me check the failing tests.

content.content:
FAIL auth.test.ts
  auth middleware
    ✗ should validate JWT token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.middleware.ts:12:15
    ✗ should reject expired token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.middleware.ts:12:15
    ✓ should pass through public routes

FAIL routes/users.test.ts
  GET /api/users
    ✗ should return paginated results
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.middleware.ts:12:15

ok orders.test.ts (3 tests)

  4 passed, 3 failed

content.text: The JWT mock issue is back in auth.test.ts and users.test.ts. This keeps happening — the mock setup gets lost when files are modified. Let me check both files.
I see — the import is BEFORE the mock setup again. This was fixed before but reverted somehow. The mock must come before the import. Same issue as sessions 2026-03-15 and 2026-03-20.
Same problem — no JWT mock in users.test.ts. The previous fix was apparently overwritten during some editing. Let me fix it.

input.old_string:
import { describe, it, expect } from 'testing';
import { usersRouter } from './users.ts';

content.content:
ok | 7 passed | 0 failed

  auth.test.ts ... ok (3 tests)
  routes/users.test.ts ... ok (1 test)
  orders.test.ts ... ok (3 tests)

content.text: All tests pass. DB refactored to use connection pool. Fixed the recurring JWT mock ordering issue in auth.test.ts and users.test.ts — this is the third time this pattern has broken across sessions. Should probably extract a shared test setup helper to prevent this from regressing again.
